title: Rich Crumbly Pressure-Cooked Slovakian Milk Custard
description: A creamy, crumbly Slovakian custard made with milk, pressure-cooked to perfection and suitable for Ayurvedic-Tamasic diet. This crowd-pleaser dessert is perfect for any occasion.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: use high-quality unbleached all-purpose flour for best results

ingredients.name: dark rum
ingredients.notes: optional, for added depth of flavor

instructions: Pour 350g of milk into the molcajete and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Whisk together 100g of all-purpose flour and 100g of cold butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add 150g of granulated sugar and mix until well combined.
Add 4 eggs, one at a time, whisking until smooth after each addition.
Add 0.5g of salt, 1g of vanilla extract and mix until combined.
Stir in 20g of almond meal and mix until well combined.
Pour the milk mixture into the molcajete and gradually add the flour mixture, whisking until smooth.
Transfer the molcajete to the pressure cooker and cook for 10 minutes.
Let the pressure drop naturally and remove the molcajete from the pressure cooker.
If using, stir in 10g of dark rum and 0.25g of cinnamon.
Pour the custard into individual serving cups and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to chill and set.
Garnish with additional almond meal and cinnamon, if desired.
